description: LACERA (Los Angeles County Employees Retirement Association) retirement plan data. Covers all 10 plans: General A/B/C/D/E/G and Safety A/B/C. Governed by the County Employees Retirement Law of 1937 (CERL) and PEPRA.
source: LACERA official plan books, basic provisions pages, COLA announcements, and actuarial reports

system_overview.name: Los Angeles County Employees Retirement Association
system_overview.governing_law: County Employees Retirement Law of 1937 (CERL), California Government Code §31450-31899.10

membership_as_of_fy2024._note: Approximate membership figures from LACERA annual reports

membership_rules.eligibility: Permanent employees of Los Angeles County and participating outside districts
membership_rules.safety_classification: Sheriff deputies, firefighters, and other safety positions as designated by LACERA
membership_rules.general_classification: All other permanent County employees not classified as safety

general.before_1979_06_01: Plan A, B, or C (based on hire date and classification)
general.1982_01_04_to_2012_11_26: Choice of Plan D or Plan E

membership_rules.membership_effective_date: First day of the month after permanent hire date

membership_dates.description: General members with membership before June 1, 1979

general_A.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 2.5% at 62 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 62; at age 62 the factor produces 100% of FAC with 40 years of service

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 30 years of County or combined reciprocal service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, derived from 10-year row of LACERA Plan A official benefit table

membership_dates.description: General members with membership before June 1, 1979

general_B.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 2.0% at 65 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 65; at age 65 the factor produces 100% of FAC with 50 years of service

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 30 years of County or combined reciprocal service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, derived from 10-year row of LACERA Plan B official benefit table

membership_dates.description: General members with membership before June 1, 1979

general_C.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 2.0% at 65 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Same formula as Plan B; age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 65

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 30 years of County or combined reciprocal service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, same formula as Plan B per LACERA basic provisions

membership_dates.description: General members hired June 1, 1979 through December 31, 2012; members hired January 4, 1982 through November 26, 2012 could choose Plan D or Plan E

general_D.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 2.0% at 65 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 65

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 30 years of County or combined reciprocal service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

cola.cola_note: COLA increases apply only to service credit earned on and after June 4, 2002

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, same formula as Plan B per LACERA basic provisions

membership_dates.description: General members hired January 4, 1982 through November 26, 2012 who elected Plan E (noncontributory option)

benefit_formula.name: 1.6% at 65 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Noncontributory plan; lower benefit factor than Plan D. Age factor increases each quarter-year from age 55 to 65

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any three 12-consecutive-month periods of service (need not be consecutive years)
final_average_compensation.fac_note: Unlike Plans A-D and G, the three years need not be consecutive

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

cola.cola_note: COLA increases apply only to service credit earned on and after June 4, 2002 and any purchased Plan E Elective COLA

disability_retirement.note: LACERA does not pay disability benefits for active Plan E members; County provides disability coverage

death_benefits_active_service.note: County pays death benefits for active Plan E members who die in service

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, derived from 10-year row of LACERA Plan E official benefit table; applies to first 35 years of service; years 36+ accrue at half rate; max benefit 80% FAC

membership_dates.description: General members hired on or after January 1, 2013 (PEPRA)

general_G.contribution_basis: Flat-rate semimonthly contribution through automatic payroll deductions

benefit_formula.name: 2.5% at 67
benefit_formula.description: PEPRA formula; age factor ranges from 1.0% at age 52 to 2.5% at age 67, increasing each quarter-year

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, from LACERA Plan G official benefit table

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of pensionable compensation earned during any 36-consecutive-month period of service

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

membership_dates.description: Safety members with membership before June 1, 1979

safety_A.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 3.0% at 55 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Safety Plan A; age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 55

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 20 years of service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, derived from LACERA Safety Plans A/B official benefit table; ages 41-49 from 20-year row, ages 50-55 from 10-year row; Safety A and B share the same benefit formula

membership_dates.description: Safety members hired June 1, 1979 through December 31, 2012

safety_B.contribution_basis: Percentage of compensation earnable, based on LACERA entry age

benefit_formula.name: 3.0% at 55 equivalent
benefit_formula.description: Safety Plan B; same formula as Safety Plan A. Age factor increases each quarter-year from age 50 to 55

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of compensation earnable during any 12-consecutive-month period of service

service_only.note: Any age with 20 years of service credit

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, derived from LACERA Safety Plans A/B official benefit table; ages 41-49 from 20-year row, ages 50-55 from 10-year row; Safety A and B share the same benefit formula

membership_dates.description: Safety members hired on or after January 1, 2013 (PEPRA)

benefit_formula.name: 2.7% at 57
benefit_formula.description: PEPRA safety formula; age factor ranges from 2.0% at age 50 to 2.7% at age 57, increasing each quarter-year

final_average_compensation.description: Highest monthly average of pensionable compensation earned during any 36-consecutive-month period of service

age_only.note: Regardless of years of service credit

healthcare_tier.exception: Members with membership dates January 1, 2013 through June 30, 2014 have Tier 1 benefits

benefit_factor_table._description: Per-year-of-service factor by age at retirement, from LACERA Safety Plan C official benefit table; PEPRA formula with clean 0.1% annual increments

unmodified.description: Highest monthly retirement allowance with a fixed eligible survivor allowance percentage

option_1.description: Reduced monthly retirement allowance with a customizable eligible survivor allowance percentage

option_2.description: Reduced monthly retirement allowance; named beneficiary receives 100% of reduced allowance upon member death

option_3.description: Reduced monthly retirement allowance; named beneficiary receives 50% of reduced allowance upon member death

option_4.description: Reduced monthly retirement allowance; named beneficiary receives a fixed percentage or dollar amount upon member death

option_lump_sum.description: Reduced monthly retirement allowance; named beneficiary receives lump-sum payment of member contributions upon member death

reciprocity.description: LACERA has reciprocal agreements with other California public retirement systems. Members who transfer between reciprocal systems can link their service for benefit calculation purposes.
reciprocal_systems: Other qualifying California public retirement systems

tier_1.subsidy: Monthly premium subsidy covering member and dependents

tier_2.eligibility: County employees hired on or after July 1, 2014
tier_2.subsidy: Monthly premium subsidy covering member only (retiree-only coverage)
tier_2.medicare_requirement: Medicare-eligible members must enroll in Medicare and a LACERA-administered Medicare healthcare plan

death_benefits.burial_benefit_note: Payable upon death of retired member unless later employed by another CERL system

irc_limits.replacement_benefit_plan: County-funded RB Plan pays the portion of the LACERA benefit that exceeds IRC §415(b) limits

notes: LACERA is governed by the County Employees Retirement Law of 1937 (CERL) and is the largest county retirement system in the United States.
Plans A, B, and C are legacy plans closed to new members since 1979; very few active members remain.
Plan D and Plan E were available concurrently from January 4, 1982 through November 26, 2012; members chose between the contributory (D) and noncontributory (E) options.
Plan G (general PEPRA) and Safety Plan C (safety PEPRA) were created effective January 1, 2013 per the Public Employees Pension Reform Act of 2013.
Safety members are generally not covered by Social Security; general members have mixed SS coverage depending on their position and agency.
COLA is based on the CPI-U for Los Angeles-Long Beach-Anaheim metro area, rounded to the nearest half percent as prescribed by law.
The STAR COLA program provides supplemental benefits for long-retired members whose purchasing power has eroded beyond plan COLA caps.
Plan E COLA only applies to service credit earned on and after June 4, 2002, plus any purchased Elective COLA.
Members who were covered under Social Security as County employees prior to January 1983 may have their retirement allowance reduced by a fixed amount for each year of SS-covered service.
PEPRA compensation limits for 2026: $159,733 (SS-covered positions) and $191,679 (non-SS-covered positions).
